The property comprises multiple building structures distributed across the 27,928 sqm plot, all constructed in modern Cycladic aesthetic. The complex includes dedicated accommodation buildings, central service facilities, staff housing, and owner residence.

Market Context

Paros Tourism Growth

Paros has emerged as one of the fastest-growing destinations in the Cyclades, with tourist arrivals increasing by an average of 12% annually over the past five years. Proximity to Naoussa (6km) positions the property in a prime catchment area.

Season Extension

The operating season has extended from 4 to 6+ months, driven by improved infrastructure and international marketing efforts positioning Paros as a year-round destination. Private church enables events outside peak season.

Demand vs Supply

Quality accommodation inventory remains limited relative to demand, creating favorable conditions for well-positioned properties with strong operational fundamentals. 58 rooms with diverse configurations serve multiple market segments.

Infrastructure Advantages

Private borehole, generator backup, and 5kw PV system provide operational independence and cost control. Transfer bus and EOT rental license create additional revenue streams.

Current Operations

Operating Model

  • • Family-operated business with established local partnerships
  • • Seasonal operation: May through October
  • • Direct bookings and OTA distribution channels
  • • On-site management and housekeeping staff
  • • Restaurant & bar operations
  • • Event hosting (weddings/baptisms)

Mix of sea/pool/garden views commands premium pricing.

Operating Expenses

Available upon NDA

Detailed operating cost structure for 3,750 sqm facility including utilities offset by private borehole, 5kw PV system, and 125kva generator. Includes payroll analysis for multi-building operation and grounds maintenance for 22,000 sqm.

Available upon NDA

5-year financial projections under multiple scenarios including room repositioning to 4-star, event venue activation, and rental operation expansion. Sensitivity analysis for 58-key inventory optimization.

Valuation Summary

Available upon NDA

Comprehensive valuation analysis for 27,928 sqm land with 3,750 sqm built area across 6 buildings. Includes cap rate comparables for Cycladic properties, replacement cost assessment, and DCF models factoring unique assets: private church, olive grove, and energy infrastructure.

Investment Thesis

1. Market Timing & Scale

Paros hospitality market at inflection point. 58-room scale with 3,750 sqm built area on 27,928 sqm provides operational efficiency and expansion potential vs smaller competitors.

2. Unique Asset Mix

Private church with 1,500 sqm reception area creates differentiated event venue. 22,000 sqm gardens with 180 olive trees, tennis, mini-golf, and outdoor chess provide experiential inventory beyond typical hotels.

3. Infrastructure Independence

Private borehole with Aegean Region license, 2 wells, 5kw PV system, and 125kva generator reduce utility dependency and operating costs. 3-phase power supports commercial equipment.

4. Multiple Revenue Streams

Rooms (58 keys), F&B (850 sqm restaurant), events (church + reception), rentals (EOT license), transfers (13+1 bus), and grounds activities. View mix (sea/pool/garden) enables tiered pricing.

Risk Factors

Seasonality

Cycladic hospitality inherently seasonal. Mitigation: Private church enables shoulder season events, EOT rentals provide year-round potential, transfer services diversify revenue.

Operational Complexity

6 buildings, 22,000 sqm grounds, and multiple facilities require skilled management. Addressed through professional operator engagement and existing staff transition.

Location Clarification

NOT beachfront. 6km from Naoussa. Some rooms have sea views. Guest expectation management critical in marketing. Offset by tranquil grounds and pool facilities.

Capex Requirements

58-room inventory across 6 buildings may require phased renovation. Mitigated by strong infrastructure (private water, generator, PV) reducing utility capex needs.

Due Diligence Materials

Following NDA execution, qualified buyers will receive access to comprehensive due diligence package including:

Historical financial statements for all 6 buildings
